office_does: One of 49 Washington State Senators, serving four-year terms writing state law and budgets for the 36th District (Ballard, Magnolia, Queen Anne, Greenwood area of northwest Seattle).
race_blurb: The clearest partisan contrast among the light races: incumbent Democrat Noel Frame, the Legislature's leading progressive-tax architect, versus Republican party officer Jillian England, whose platform is built on repealing the Millionaires Tax and the Climate Commitment Act. Both advance to the general in this two-candidate race.

candidates.summary: Incumbent State Senator since 2023 after seven years as the district's State Representative; Vice Chair of Finance on Senate Ways & Means and the Legislature's most prominent advocate for progressive tax restructuring.
highlights: Prime sponsor of Washington's proposed wealth tax since 2021 (SB 5486, SB 5797) and helped pass the capital gains tax and the 2026 'Millionaires Tax' [S1][S3][S4]
Cites expansion of the Working Families Tax Credit to 460,000 new households, a senior property tax exemption, and low-income energy assistance [S1]
Ten years in the Legislature, with finance-committee leadership in both chambers (past House Finance chair, current Ways & Means Vice Chair of Finance) [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: Prime sponsor of the wealth tax since 2021 and helped pass the capital gains tax and Millionaires Tax; calls the tax code 'upside-down' and regressive.

spending.basis: Platform is built on raising revenue to fund schools, healthcare, human services, and early learning, with expanded credit and assistance programs.

experience.basis: Ten-year incumbent legislator with committee leadership in both chambers and a signature enacted tax record.

candidates.summary: Republican challenger and longtime party precinct officer with a career in computer science and healthcare business operations, running on repealing the Millionaires Tax and the Climate Commitment Act.
highlights: Top priority is repealing the 9.9% 'Millionaires Tax,' which she characterizes as a door to broader income taxation; also targets the sales tax and B&O tax [S1][S2]
Would repeal the Climate Commitment Act, citing 50-65 cents per gallon in gas costs, and supports an 'all-of-the-above' energy strategy including nuclear and natural gas [S1][S2]
Calls for accountability on homelessness, clearing encampments, and 'breaking feedback loops' between addiction, migration, and policy tolerance [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: Platform centers on repealing the Millionaires Tax and reducing the sales tax and B&O tax.

spending.basis: Repeated emphasis on accountability, cost-efficiency, and deregulation; states 'government force cannot create prosperity, safety, or abundance.'

safety.basis: Calls for clearing encampments, accountability, and breaking addiction-policy 'feedback loops' — an enforcement-and-accountability approach to street disorder.

climate.basis: Would repeal the Climate Commitment Act on gas-price grounds and expand nuclear, natural gas, and hydroelectric energy.

experience.basis: No prior elected office beyond party precinct officer; runs on private-sector cost-efficiency experience.

social.basis: Says schools should 'educate students rather than indoctrinate them' and demands accountability — parental-rights-inflected framing on curricula.
